haiku:
El Niño returns
Eighty percent by August
Guterres calls urgent

Data centers eat
Four forty-eight terawatt hours
AI drinking rivers

Hyperspectral wave
Pixxel Wyvern Kuva Space
The SAR playbook fails

Copernicus CHIME
Twenty twenty-eight milestone
Hyperspectral real

Taos Plaza Live
July twenty-three returns
Free music Thursdays

ICE seventy bil
Funded through twenty-nine now
Sweeping House two twelve

Gorge at first light
Two hundred elk on the rim
Sage smoke rising slow

Red Sox versus Jays
Tampa series after that
June baseball perfect

Patriots offseason
Drake Maye waits for September
Fox still on the wire

Telemark dawn line
Kachina untracked powder
One turn from the wire

Pentagon lockdown
hazmat teams sweep the hallways
gas prices climb west

Knicks down twenty nine
Madison Square finds a way
Spurs feel winter now

Eleven hottest
years on record, Guterres
says what we all know

Hyperspectral moves
from demo to ops at last
Pixxel counts pixels

El Niño returns
eighty percent by August
Guterres warns fast

Taos Canyon burns
crews hold the line by Wednesday
June wind, sage, and char

Pueblo powwow gone
infrastructure cannot hold
Kit Carson dust dry

Inflation past four
Hormuz chokes the flow again
pump price tells the truth

Wheeler Peak holds snow
June eleven, still a cap
telemark in July

Two fifty years gone
starship launched a million strong
data centers drift

Ocean temps broke June
El Niño returns again
Coastal Carbon knows

Heat dome July Fourth
two hundred million people
Boston sweats it out

Trump pardons those who
fixed their own car emissions
clean air act reversed

Khamenei casket
millions chant for revenge now
Tehran funeral

Red Sox beat Angels
five to two last night Chapman
relief record set

Chestnut sixty-six
Coney Island champion
eighteenth hot dog crown

Aurlandsdalen hut
Oskar graduates next May
Vilde waits in peaks

Redhead at the ranch
dust on her boots, Mesa beer
stars over Wheeler

The dashboard opens
clean ranked queue, haiku unfold
wire holds for now

SpaceX one million
orbital compute vision
where does data live

ICEYE credit line
three fifty million euros
SAR intelligence

Thermal satellite
SatLeo monitors cities
heat islands mapped

Planet Germany
two sixty million contract
defense EO won

Khamenei's second day
Tehran funeral stretches long
chants for revenge rise

Coney Island fence
black mask fires into cookout
six and seven years

Sox power still gone
thirteen bombs, dead last again
walks won't save this year

El Niño's back now
rapid strengthening they say
Guterres warns hard

Data centers switch
backup generators humming
heat wave frees the grid

Taos Plaza Live
July twenty-three it starts
Thursday nights till August

Burn ban holds in Taos
no fireworks for July Fourth
Mavericks played instead

Eagle and Falcon
NASA's new earth watchers fly
spectral mapper launched

Red Willow at dusk
Mavericks and Felix played
no fireworks this year

OMB proposes
federal grants reshaped soon
October first live

Monsoon should be here
pinyon dust and waiting sky
July still too dry

ATS job board glows
three roles, none quite right today
refresh one more time

Khamenei's funeral
Trump says we'll win either way
Tehran chants for blood

Typhoon Bavi spins
Guam braces for the big wind
Pacific islands drown

Sox host Rays this week
July seventeenth first pitch
Fenway summer night

Nonfarm jobs fifty
seven thousand way below
what the street wanted

Taos Plaza Live starts
July twenty third at six
Nosotros plays free

Wheeler Peak still white
mid-July monsoons building
heat dome ninety-four

Red Sox eight thirteen
Patriots Day comeback win
Yankees arrive Tuesday

Sudan drone war kills
three hundred children six months
UNICEF counts the dead

The dashboard refreshed
haiku hold the wire again
clean queue earns the rest

Strait of Hormuz chokes
tankers under attack today
oil still moves north

Patriots train camp
Maye and Brown take the practice field
Super Bowl hangover

Cubs blackout complete
island power grid collapsed
third time this year

NATO summit tense
Trump arrives, Europe balks
Iran rift deepens

Khamenei buried
Tehran funeral procession
war or talk unclear

Swift marries Kelce
Madison Square Garden vows
guests leak every word

Monsoon afternoon
thirty-three degrees at noon
thunderstorms by four

Red Sox at the Mets
July road trip continues
Fenway dark tonight

Great Barrier Reef
worst coral die-off on record
El Niño strengthens

Two satellites up
Carbon Mapper tracks the plumes
oil patch can't hide

Macron in Damascus
Four Seasons hotel bombed
state visit continues
intros: Clean queue. Haiku hold the wire.
Taos Plaza Live starts Thursday. Gazebo, six-thirty. Althea's Secret and Nosotros.
Las Fiestas de Taos starts Thursday July 17. Burn ban still in effect.
Great Barrier Reef logged worst coral die-off on record. Super El Niño still building.
Trump says U.S. will win one way or the other as Iran buries Khamenei.
The pipeline will refill. Until then:
Typhoon Bavi hammering Guam. Super El Niño still building across Pacific.
No offers to chew on. A haiku to sit with.
All quiet on the pipeline front. Haiku dispatch:
Tier 1: empty. Tier 2: empty. Tier haiku: full.
The dashboard rests. The poet does not.
Scoreboard's blank today. Let the poem fill the hole.
Your pipeline called in sick today. Haiku showed up instead.
Between waves. A haiku to hold the space.
The mesa wind ran the screener today. Nothing made the cut except this.
The ATS ate them all. The haiku survived.
Trump signed the deep-sea mining order Friday. ISA gets bypassed, the seabed gets sold by decree. Pipeline ran quiet anyway. Haiku held.
MethaneSAT went dark in orbit. CBAM hit seventy-five a ton. Your old world keeps shifting. Pipeline quiet. Haiku shows up.
Brussels punted EUDR another year. The pipeline did not blink. Haiku holds.
Tanager-1 caught ten methane leaks for California's air board. Microsoft buys ninety percent of the carbon market by itself now. Pipeline quiet. Haiku stretches.
Virginia redrew four House seats in a single referendum. Pipeline did not flinch. Haiku keeps count.
Cora got fired Saturday night, ten and seventeen out of the gate. Tracy ran the bench Sunday and won five to three behind a Contreras homer. Pipeline ran quiet. Haiku showed up.
Sangres east at one percent of normal snowpack. Palo Flechado at four. Acequias rationing in Talpa already. The water year is the story now. Pipeline quiet. Haiku holds.
EDF and fifteen co-petitioners filed against the EPA endangerment repeal April sixteenth. The litigation calendar is the work now. Pipeline quiet. Haiku on the wall.
Patriots took Caleb Lomu in the first round. Trench got an adult. Pipeline did not. Haiku covers the gap.
Artemis 2 came back from the moon April first, four humans, fifty years between. Pipeline quiet that week too. Haiku still here.
